text:
Bottom line

Leadership should not authorize the $48.7 million redemption wire or send a final NAV/cash/payment-date commitment to Crestline before the 16:30 ET pre-read unless authority gates actually pass. The frozen packet does not evidence that they have. The right posture is a controlled hold: send a Legal- and Fund-Operations-reviewed limited holding notice, prepare the wire package without release, and escalate every open dependency. The core rule is simple: urgency is not authority, and reversible readiness is not irreversible release. The LPA calculates redemption proceeds only after the Administrator delivers the official NAV statement and the valuation committee accepts the official strike, and release requires documented CFO and COO, or delegate, approval after the official package is complete [S1_FUND_LPA_REDEMPTION_NAV_AUTHORITY]. Treasury policy separately bars releasing a redemption wire over $10 million from an estimated NAV table, preliminary administrator comment, portfolio-manager urgency note, or investor-relations workbook [S4_TREASURY_WIRE_RELEASE_CONTROL_POLICY]. This does not foreclose same-day release if documentary gates arrive; it forecloses release on the current evidence.

Risks of acting

Immediate release would outrun the authority record. The wire amount comes from a derived table that is not independent authority, official NAV, board approval, administrator signoff, cash-control approval, or proof that liquidity gates are satisfied [S3_DERIVED_NAV_LIQUIDITY_TABLE]. The proposed final note is also unsafe: Legal has not approved language saying the NAV and cash amount are “final and releasing today,” and Compliance says no final cash amount, NAV, or payment date should be sent until the official package, side-letter review, liquidity-gate analysis, and Treasury approvals are confirmed [S7_LEGAL_COMPLIANCE_REVIEW_EXCERPT]. The side letter increases the consequence of premature wording because a final cash/payment-date commitment must not be materially inconsistent with the official NAV package absent correction, error, or gating event [S5_INVESTOR_SIDE_LETTER_LIQUIDITY_GATE_NOTICE].

The economics are not mere process points. Estimated proceeds of $48.7 million against $53.2 million immediately available leave about a $4.5 million cushion, or 1.1% of estimated NAV, if the $12.0 million subscription receivable does not settle; with that receivable, the cushion is about $16.5 million, or 4.0% [S3_DERIVED_NAV_LIQUIDITY_TABLE]. Both are below the 8.0% internal dashboard target, which requires current LPA gate review but is not proven to be a binding LPA threshold [S3_DERIVED_NAV_LIQUIDITY_TABLE; S1_FUND_LPA_REDEMPTION_NAV_AUTHORITY]. The controller expects the receivable only on the next banking day, making the thinner cushion the relevant near-term case absent approved contrary treatment [S10_FUND_CONTROLLER_MARK_DISPUTE_NOTE]. Two unresolved marks carry downside sensitivity of $9.8 million and upside of $2.4 million; mechanically applying Crestline’s 11.8% interest implies roughly $1.16 million downside and $0.28 million upside impact on proceeds, but that is only an illustrative sensitivity because official NAV and capital-account support are absent [S3_DERIVED_NAV_LIQUIDITY_TABLE; S10_FUND_CONTROLLER_MARK_DISPUTE_NOTE; S2_ADMIN_PRELIMINARY_NAV_EMAIL]. Acting now risks overpayment, underpayment, impaired remaining-investor liquidity, and a later correction that damages trust.

Risks of waiting

Waiting also has costs. Crestline expects a status update for its 16:30 pre-read; portfolio management says missing the window puts a $75 million co-invest allocation at risk [S6_PORTFOLIO_MANAGER_URGENCY_NOTE]; and Legal recognizes that silence may create investor-relations and side-letter friction [S7_LEGAL_COMPLIANCE_REVIEW_EXCERPT]. The side letter requires a same-day status update when a redemption may be gated, deferred, or paid on a materially different timetable [S5_INVESTOR_SIDE_LETTER_LIQUIDITY_GATE_NOTICE]. So silence is not the recommendation. The answer is active narrow communication plus reversible readiness: reduce friction without asserting unproven finality. The $75 million allocation is “at risk,” not certainly lost, and the packet does not show that release today preserves it [S6_PORTFOLIO_MANAGER_URGENCY_NOTE].

The strongest counterargument is that pro-release evidence is credible: the administrator says no single reconciliation break currently exceeds 25 bps, the table shows enough immediately available cash before release, Crestline is a flagship investor, and the retired playbook once permitted estimated-NAV release for relationship-sensitive investors under a 50 bps variance [S2_ADMIN_PRELIMINARY_NAV_EMAIL; S3_DERIVED_NAV_LIQUIDITY_TABLE; S6_PORTFOLIO_MANAGER_URGENCY_NOTE; S8_STALE_REDEMPTION_PLAYBOOK_CONTEXT]. But that evidence supports urgency, escalation, wire preparation, and a holding notice—not authority. The administrator calls the worksheet preliminary and says official NAV is expected tomorrow morning after final signoff, not delivered [S2_ADMIN_PRELIMINARY_NAV_EMAIL]. The playbook was superseded after liquidity-gate revisions and cannot override current LPA, Treasury policy, side letter, or legal review [S8_STALE_REDEMPTION_PLAYBOOK_CONTEXT]. The weak IR explainer favors speed but does not distinguish holding notices from final commitments [S9_WEAK_INVESTOR_RELATIONS_BLOG_CONTEXT].

Next steps / stop-go gates

Execute the required options as a trigger taxonomy.

Broad no-go: do_not_release_cash_from_estimated_nav_or_urgency_alone — do not wire from the derived table, preliminary email, or relationship pressure [S1_FUND_LPA_REDEMPTION_NAV_AUTHORITY; S4_TREASURY_WIRE_RELEASE_CONTROL_POLICY]. do_not_send_final_nav_or_cash_commitment_until_authority_gates_pass — do not call NAV, cash amount, or payment date final until official package, Legal/Fund-Operations review, side-letter and gate analysis, and Treasury approvals are complete [S5_INVESTOR_SIDE_LETTER_LIQUIDITY_GATE_NOTICE; S7_LEGAL_COMPLIANCE_REVIEW_EXCERPT].

Narrow/conditional go now: prepare_wire_package_without_release — build templates, callback logs, bank screens, and release checklists, with no funds moved [S4_TREASURY_WIRE_RELEASE_CONTROL_POLICY]. draft_limited_holding_notice_for_legal_and_operations_review — issue only language saying the NAV package and gate analysis remain in process, with no final NAV, cash amount, or payment date, after Legal/Fund-Operations clearance [S7_LEGAL_COMPLIANCE_REVIEW_EXCERPT].

Hold/escalate: reconcile_administrator_marks_and_subscription_receivable — resolve the two marks and receivable timing [S2_ADMIN_PRELIMINARY_NAV_EMAIL; S10_FUND_CONTROLLER_MARK_DISPUTE_NOTE]. verify_side_letter_liquidity_gate_and_notice_constraints — confirm how the notice clause and current LPA gate language affect timing and content [S5_INVESTOR_SIDE_LETTER_LIQUIDITY_GATE_NOTICE; S1_FUND_LPA_REDEMPTION_NAV_AUTHORITY]. obtain_required_administrator_valuation_treasury_legal_approvals — secure official NAV, valuation acceptance, Legal sign-off, the LPA CFO-and-COO approval, and the separate Treasury-plus-CFO-or-COO release approval, with delegate authority documented [S1_FUND_LPA_REDEMPTION_NAV_AUTHORITY; S4_TREASURY_WIRE_RELEASE_CONTROL_POLICY].

Expansion gate: release cash and send a final notice only when official administrator NAV and capital-account support are delivered, valuation acceptance is documented, marks and receivable treatment are resolved, liquidity-gate and side-letter review are complete, Legal approves the language, and both approval regimes are satisfied.

Revoke/stop: halt if any gate owner lacks documentary completion, the receivable remains unsettled without approved treatment, marks remain NAV-material, or liquidity is not recalculated under current LPA gate language. Before release, park templates and withdraw unsent drafts; after release, the issue becomes remediation, not rollback [S4_TREASURY_WIRE_RELEASE_CONTROL_POLICY; S10_FUND_CONTROLLER_MARK_DISPUTE_NOTE].

Post-action follow-up: document_stop_go_triggers_for_cash_release_and_investor_notice — keep a time-stamped log marking each gate “complete with evidence,” “open,” or “not applicable with rationale,” never a single informal “ready” flag, because “operationally ready” is not authority [S3_DERIVED_NAV_LIQUIDITY_TABLE].

Claim boundaries

This brief does not conclude that the official administrator NAV statement has been delivered, the valuation committee accepted the official strike, the two private credit marks are resolved, the $12.0 million subscription receivable has settled, liquidity-gate review or side-letter interpretation is complete, Legal approved the proposed final note, Treasury release approval exists, CFO/COO or delegate approvals are documented, sanctions or eligibility refresh occurred, or investor consent was obtained. Each remains open or unevidenced in the frozen packet. It also does not conclude that waiting will lose the $75 million allocation, release would preserve it, a side-letter breach has occurred, or the 8.0% dashboard figure is a binding LPA threshold. The liquidity and mark figures are estimates and illustrative sensitivities, not official redemption calculations. This is a benchmark artifact and not legal advice, investment advice, fund administration advice, accounting advice, regulatory advice, investor communication approval, or payment authorization.
